releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description: ⚡ Performance Improvements Zero-Lag Hardware Acceleration: We completely rewrote the doodle extraction engine. Moving and cutting drawings on the massive 5000x5000 canvas no longer drops GPU acceleration. The canvas stays buttery smooth even during heavy drawing sessions. Asynchronous Smart Saving: Replaced the heavy, synchronous saving mechanism with a new Background Blob Engine. Your canvas now saves silently in the background without freezing Obsidian or interrupting your drawing flow. ✨ Features & Enhancements Style Selection: When you use the lasso tool to select and move a drawing, it now behaves exactly as you expect. It creates a pure, transparent pixel sticker instead of morphing into a clunky text card with borders and buttons. Seamless Re-stamping: Dropping a moved drawing now flawlessly stamps it back onto the canvas ink layer, leaving no ghost HTML nodes behind. 🐛 Bug Fixes Fixed the "Phantom Card" Glitch: Resolved an issue where moving a drawing would leave a permanent, empty blue-bordered card on the canvas. Data Loss Prevention (Race Condition Fix): Fixed a critical bug where moving a drawing and immediately closing the file would cause the drawing to vanish forever. The canvas now features a "Life Insurance" protocol that forces a synchronous safety save right before the tab closes, ensuring no stroke is ever lost.
releases.name: V4.8.2 - The Zero-Lag Canvas

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description: Welcome to the biggest update of Cornell Marginalia yet! We are taking your notes out of the linear scrolling paradigm and bringing them into a fully spatial, two-dimensional thinking environment. Introducing the Pinboard View — an infinite, zero-lag spatial canvas built directly into Obsidian, designed specifically for your marginalia, evidence boards, and active recall sessions. ✨ What's New: Core Features Drag & Drop Materialization: Drag any marginalia or group directly onto the infinite canvas. Cards will automatically expand to fit their content, perfectly rendering Markdown, internal links, and PDF++ crops. Semantic Stitching: Connect your thoughts visually. Click the "Link" icon on any card to draw a connection to another note. You can add semantic labels (e.g., "contradicts", "supports") to define the exact relationship between ideas. Interactive Smart Lines: Your connections are alive. Scroll your mouse wheel over any line to dynamically change its thickness. Click on a line to open the Style Menu, where you can change its color or switch styles (Solid, Dashed, Long Dashes, or Morse Code). Super Doodle Engine with Smart Ink 🧠: Draw freely on the board with zero lag thanks to our hardware-accelerated Event Coalescing engine. Best of all? The new Smart Ink brush automatically adapts to your Obsidian theme, flipping from black to white seamlessly if you switch between Light and Dark modes. Spatial Flashcard Review (Spaced Repetition): Turn your canvas into a study theater. Click the 🧠 Brain icon in the top right to scan your entire board for active recall cards. It automatically extracts your hidden questions and citations, opening a distraction-free, floating review modal with keyboard support (Spacebar to flip, Enter to advance). Blueprint Camera (Native Export): Export your entire board as a high-resolution PNG with a single click. Our custom rendering engine takes a clean "blueprint" snapshot of your cards, doodles, and arrows, perfectly wrapping text and stripping away UI clutter for a professional export. Sticky Notes & Context Expansion: Need to add a quick thought? Click the Sticky Note icon to drop a free-text card anywhere. Double-click to edit it with full Markdown support. Need more info from a dragged note? Click the "Quote" icon to instantly materialize its surrounding context right next to it. 🕹️ How to Navigate (Controls & Shortcuts) We built the Pinboard to feel natural and fluid. Here is how to move around your new mind palace: Pan the Canvas: Hold Spacebar + Left Click and drag (or just use the Middle Mouse Button) to move around the board. Zoom In/Out: Hold Ctrl (or Cmd on Mac) + Mouse Wheel to zoom smoothly into your cursor's location. Marquee Selection: Click and drag on the empty background to draw a selection box. Select multiple cards at once to move them as a group. Delete Items: Select one or multiple cards and press Delete or Backspace to instantly remove them (and their attached arrows) from the board. This does not delete your original notes from the Obsidian vault. Doodle Controls: Use the floating toolbar at the bottom to switch between the Hand tool (navigation), Pen, and Eraser. Adjust your stroke thickness using the integrated slider.
releases.name: V4.8.1 - The Infinite Pinboard Update 🗺️✨

releases.author: latazadehomero

How it works: When activated, simply hover your mouse over any highlight or image crop inside your PDF. A sleek, floating popover will instantly appear, displaying the exact marginalia linked to that specific annotation. Fully Interactive: The floating popovers aren't just text—they are full UI components! You can click the Pin to Board button directly from the PDF overlay, and your Sidebar/Evidence Board will update instantly in real-time.

Fixed the "double reveal" bug in PDFs.

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
### 🚀 New Features

#### New Filter: Show Only flashcards 

#### 🔗 Multi-Marginalia Support (`;;` Delimiter)
The `OmniCapture` engine is now smarter. By using the `;;` (double semicolon) delimiter in your capture prompt, the plugin will automatically split your input into distinct marginalia blocks.

* **Flashcard + Context:** Create a question and its personal explanation in one go.
* **Sequential Thoughts:** Capture multiple ideas related to the same clipboard context, and they will be injected as individual, clean blocks.

### 📝 How it works
When you trigger the capture (Alt+C), simply separate your thoughts with `;;`. 

**Example Input:**
> `What occurs in the mitochondria?;; This is why it generates ATP`

**Generated Output:**
```markdown
%%> What occurs in the mitochondria?;; %%
%%> This is why it generates ATP %%
```
releases.name: V4.7.8 - Multi-Marginalia Capture

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
Safer Templater Integration: We've upgraded how the plugin interacts with Templater. 

Enhanced File System Safety: The OmniCapture  engines now strictly filter invalid or weird characters from file names, ensuring your vault's structure remains perfectly clean and safe.

UI Stability: We've replaced several older rendering methods with Obsidian's native, modern API. This prevents notes with strange titles from breaking the interface in the Rhizome Canvas or Sidebar Tooltips.
releases.name: V4.7.7 - Security & Stability Update

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description: ⚠️ Important Note: To access all these new features, remember that the Dashboard must be activated from the plugin Settings first! Once enabled, you can open it using the ribbon icon or the command palette. ✨ New Features 📎 Manual Note Attachments in Syllabus: You can now manually attach specific notes to a Subject's Topic using the new + button. These attached notes bypass strict text-matching rules and are automatically included in your Review and Cram sessions. 📋 Weekly Planner Clipboard (Copy/Paste): Building your weekly routine is now lightning fast. Added "Copy" and "Paste" buttons to the day columns, allowing you to clone an entire day's routine (e.g., "Optimal Monday") and inject it into other days with a single click. 🧠 Smart Form Memory: The Weekly Planner's "Add Block" modal now has short-term memory. It remembers your last selected day and automatically sets the next block's start time to the previous block's end time (auto-incrementing by 1 hour). No more repetitive scrolling! 📊 Analytics Redesign: Mastery vs. Confidence We completely decoupled how learning is measured to reflect real-world studying: ⚡ Mastery (SRS): Now strictly evaluates Flashcards (blocks containing ;;). It tracks New, Learning, and Mature cards based on spaced repetition intervals. 📖 Confidence (Active Reading): Now evaluates Explanatory Notes. It averages your subjective 1-10 confidence scores for notes you've read, giving you a realistic overview of your reading comprehension. Timeline Nodes Update: The Exam Timeline nodes now intelligently display either your SRS Mastery percentage or your Reading Confidence percentage, depending on the content you have prepared.

How it works: Toggle the new Brain icon (🧠) in the Marginalia Explorer. Instantly, all the highlights and rectangular crops you've made in Obsidian's native PDF viewer (and PDF++) will transform into dark, opaque blocks. Smart Spatial Engine: We've built a custom spatial algorithm. releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description: In this update, we’ve blurred the line between your notes and your primary sources. Cornell Marginalia now integrates deeply and natively with the PDF++ ecosystem, transforming your vault into a true Active Recall machine. Our Marginalia Explorer sidebar introduces two new tools designed to accelerate your study workflow: 📖 1. Direct PDF Jump (Bypass Mode) Tired of clicking a marginalia, going to the Markdown note, and then clicking again just to reach the PDF? How it works: Toggle the new Book icon (📖) in the filter bar. When you click on any marginal note connected to a document, the plugin will locate the exact quote. The result: You will be teleported instantly to the exact page, highlight, or rectangular crop within the PDF, completely bypassing the intermediate Markdown note. It works perfectly even if your links are buried inside complex Callouts! 🧠 2. Native Active Recall in PDFs (The Redaction Tape) We are bringing the power of Flashcards directly to your original documents. How it works: Toggle the Brain icon (🧠) in the Marginalia Explorer. Instantly, all the highlights and rectangular crops you've made in Obsidian's native PDF viewer (and PDF++) will transform into dark, opaque blocks. The result: Your PDFs become interactive study sessions. The highlighted text is hidden beneath our "redaction tape", allowing you to test your memory on the spot. Simply hover your mouse over the dark block to magically reveal the underlying text and its original highlight color. Additional Optimizations: Massive improvements in Blur precision within Reading Mode

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
### ✨ What's New?

#### 🛠️ Fully Customizable Omni-Capture Templates
Head over to the plugin Settings to find the new **Omni-Capture Template** text area. You can now design the exact markdown structure you want for your quick captures using native placeholders:
- `{{text}}`: Your main thought or comment.
- `{{citation}}`: The context text copied from your clipboard.
- `{{image}}`: Any clipboard images 

#### 🪄 Native Templater Support
Omni-Capture is now fully powered by **Templater**! You can include any Templater syntax (e.g., `<% tp.date.now("YYYY-MM-DD") %>`) right inside your capture template. The engine will evaluate and resolve the code instantly before saving the capture to your destination note. 

#### 🧠 Seamless Active Recall & Flashcard Integration
Want every capture from PDFs or web articles to become an instant flashcard? Just tweak your template! By adding our native `;;` syntax to your template, your captures will automatically trigger the active recall blur effect.
*Example Template:*
```markdown
{{image}}{{citation}}%%> {{text}} ;;%%
```


---
*As always, thank you for your ongoing support, feedback, and ideas. Happy knowledge harvesting! 🍅📚*
releases.name: v4.7.3 - The Omni-Capture Template Update!

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
# 🚀  The Templater Integration Update! 

We are thrilled to announce the full integration of **Cornell Marginalia** with **Templater**! 

This update completely transforms how you extract, connect, and export your knowledge. Now, all your templates and Drag & Drop actions natively support the powerful Templater syntax (`<% ... %>`)—instantly and friction-free.

### ✨ Main Features

#### 🛸 Surgical Drag & Drop 
**Dragging marginalias from the Marginalia Explorer into an active note or a Canvas** is now smarter than ever! Whenever you drag and drop, all these features trigger automatically
- **Instant Resolution:** If your Drag & Drop template contains Templater commands (e.g., `<% tp.date.now() %>`), they will be automatically resolved the exact moment you drop the note into the editor.
- **Multi-Level Support:** It works for everything! You can drag a single note, a **full Semantic Thread with all its children**, or a **Folder Super-Box**, and the engine will process every Templater tag in cascade.


#### 🗂️ Universal Exports Powered by Templater
All export engines now batch-process your information through Templater before generating the final file. You can use dynamic variables in:
- **📌 Pinboard to Markdown:** When compiling your active board into a new note, all individual item templates and the main document template will pass through Templater.
- **🎨 Evidence Board (Obsidian Canvas):** Cards generated on the Canvas now fully support dynamic metadata resolution.


### ⚙️ How to get started?
1. Make sure you have the **Templater** plugin installed and enabled in your vault.
2. Go to your **Cornell Marginalia Settings**.
3. In your export templates or your *Drag & Drop* template, simply mix our native variables with Templater's. 
   *Example Drag & Drop Template:*

   ```markdown
- {{text}} 
	- > {{citation}}
	- 🔗 Source: {{source_note}}
	- 📅 Extracted on: <% tp.file.creation_date() %>
   ```



---
*As always, thank you for your ongoing support and feedback. Happy knowledge harvesting! 🍅📚*
releases.name: V4.7.2  - Templater Integration Update

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description: We're bringing major structural improvements and new integrations to streamline your workflow! * 📁 **Vault Folders & Notes (Major Update):** Added a new view button in the *Vault* tab. You can now visually organize all your marginalias in collapsible boxes, hierarchically structured by **Folder > File**. It includes smart rendering (*Lazy Loading*) to navigate through thousands of notes with **zero lag**, and inherits all customization features (custom colors and pinning). * 🎨 **Excalidraw Split-Drop (Ctrl + Drag):** New integration! Hold down `Ctrl` (or `Cmd`) while dragging a marginalia. The mouse will only carry the clean text to the canvas, while the original content (like your PDF++ citation) is automatically copied to your clipboard. Just drop the text and press `Ctrl+V` to get both elements separated in seconds! * 🧠 **Smart Contextual Drag & Drop:** We completely refined the dragging logic. The system now understands exactly where you are dragging from: in the *Vault* tab, isolated individual notes are respected; in *Threads*, you carry the entire branch of child notes; and full boxes strictly preserve their hierarchy when moved. * 🖼️**Fixed Explorer Backgrounds:** The custom background now anchors to the Obsidian leaf master. This ensures your image stays put while your notes glide over it, no matter how long your list is.
releases.name: V4.7.1 - Vault Folders

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
We've polished the core mechanics for exporting, dragging, and organizing your marginalias. Here are the latest improvements and bug fixes:

    ✨ Smart Drag & Drop Templates: Dragging individual notes from anywhere (Explorer or Pinboard) now perfectly respects your custom Markdown template ({{text}}, {{citation}}, etc.).

    🌳 Recursive Thread Dragging: Dragging a note from the Threads tab now automatically pulls all its children and sub-notes. Dragging from Current or Vault keeps the single note isolated.

    📌 Full Pinboard Hierarchy: The "Export to Board" (⏺︎) button on Semantic Boxes now successfully brings the entire thread tree into the Pinboard, keeping child notes neatly indented.

    📄 PDF++ Compatibility Fix: Fixed an issue where multiline PDF highlights or embeds were being cut off and breaking the memory cache.

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description: ### 🚀 Release v4.6.9: Multi-Marginalia Blocks, Tag Auto-Suggest & Visual Helper We are thrilled to bring you a new update focused on precision, better note-linking, and giving you more freedom when formatting your Cornell notes! Here is what's new: #### 📝 Multi-Marginalia Editorial Blocks The ````cornell` code blocks have received a major architectural upgrade. You are no longer restricted to a single margin note at the top! * You can now insert **multiple marginalias (`%%> ... %%`) anywhere** inside the block. * The rendering engine will intelligently stack them in the margin, perfectly aligned with your main text. #### 🏷️ Native Tag Auto-Suggest for Omni-Capture To supercharge the new Semantic Threads feature, we've brought Obsidian's native tag auto-completion directly into the Omni-Capture modals and sidebars. * Just type `#` in the Omni-Capture textarea, and a dropdown will instantly suggest the tags already existing in your vault. * Connecting ideas and routing your marginalias to the right semantic boxes has never been faster. #### 📍 Visual Helper (Anchor Dots) Ever lost track of exactly which word or line a long margin note belongs to? Meet the Visual Helper. * When enabled, it renders a tiny, color-coded dot (matching your tag's color) in the text exactly where the marginalia is anchored. * **Fully compatible** with both *Live Preview* and *Reading View*. * **Disabled by default** to keep your view perfectly clean. You can quickly toggle it on/off using the new map-pin (📍) icon in the left Ribbon or via the Command Palette. --- *As always, thank you for your feedback and support. Happy note-taking!* 🧠✨
releases.name: V4.6.9 - Multi-Marginalia Blocks, Tag Auto-Suggest & Visual Helper

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description: ## This update brings serious productivity to the **Marginalia Explorer**. ### 📌 1. Pinned Threads (VIP Boxes) Is a specific thread like `#urgency` or `#current-project` getting buried in your sidebar? Not anymore. * **Frictionless Pinning:** Hover over any Thread Box header, and you will now see a sleek `pin` icon next to the color palette. Click it, and the box instantly flies to the top of its level. * **Persistent Memory:** Pinned threads stay at the top across reloads and are visually separated from the rest of the boxes by a subtle divider. * **Recursive Pinning:** This works at any level! You can pin a Simple Box to the top of the Sidebar, or pin a sub-box to the top of its parent Super-Category. ### 📝 2. The "Shadow Task Manager" You can now write actionable tasks directly in the margins of your texts without breaking your reading flow. * **Interactive Sidebar:** If you write a marginalia using the task syntax (e.g., `%%> - [ ] #urgent research this concept %%`), the Explorer will automatically render it as a real, clickable checkbox in the sidebar. * **The Vaporizer (Auto-Delete):** A new setting allows you to automatically keep your vault clean. If you check a task in the sidebar, the plugin doesn't just cross it out—it completely vaporizes the entire `%%> ... %%` block from your actual Markdown file, leaving no trace behind. ### 🚀 3. TaskNotes HTTP API Integration Turn your marginaliae into a GTD powerhouse with our new native integration for the **TaskNotes** plugin. * **1-Click Export:** Hover over any task in the Explorer, and a blue `list-todo` icon will appear. Click it, and the plugin will securely connect to your local TaskNotes HTTP server, extract your tags, and send the purified task title directly to your database. * **Auto-Configuration:** No need to type ports or tokens. Our bridge automatically reads your TaskNotes configuration (`data.json`) and establishes a secure connection. ### 🔄 4. Zero-Lag Symmetrical Sync (On-Demand) We hate background processes that drain your RAM and battery. That's why our TaskNotes synchronization is **100% On-Demand**. * **Smart Refresh:** We hijacked the existing Refresh (`⟳`) button in the sidebar. When you click it, the plugin quickly asks TaskNotes: *"Did I complete any of these tasks over there?"* * If a task was marked as done in TaskNotes, the Marginalia Explorer will instantly tick it (or vaporize it) in your local Markdown files. Perfect synchronization with zero background lag! ### ⚙️ New Settings Available * **Auto-Delete Completed Tasks:** Toggle whether checking a task destroys the marginalia or just marks it as `[x]`. * **TaskNotes HTTP API Integration:** Enable this to show the export buttons and activate the On-Demand Sync. --- *Stay focused, stay organized! 🧠✨*
releases.name: v4.6.8 - Pinned Threads & The Shadow Task Manager 📌✅

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description: Welcome to the biggest update to the Marginalia Explorer. We are transforming how you organize knowledge by introducing Semantic Threads: a visual, auto-generating, and polyhierarchical Zettelkasten engine right inside your sidebar. Say goodbye to messy lists and hello to interactive thought clusters. # 📦 1. Semantic Threads: How it Works The Explorer now automatically reads the #tags inside your marginaliae and generates visual "Boxes" (Threads) to group them. If you write %%> #ideas This is a brilliant thought %%, the system instantly creates an "Ideas" box in your sidebar and places your note inside. Polyhierarchy (Intersectionality): Real thoughts don't live in a single folder. If you write %%> #ideas #projects/webapp Brainstorming the new UI %%, the system will dynamically clone your note and display it in both the "Ideas" and "Projects/Webapp" boxes simultaneously. Updating or moving one will safely update the exact tag without breaking the others. # 🏛️ 2. The Taxonomy (Hierarchy Levels) Your threads are now organized in a strict, logical hierarchy: Untagged: Your Inbox. Notes floating without a specific tag. Simple Box: Created by a single base tag (e.g., #ideas or #planning). Major Box (Super-Category): A parent container holding multiple sub-boxes (e.g., #projects/webapp and #projects/mobile-app). # 🧲 3. The Laws of Zettelkasten Gravity (Drag & Drop) Organizing your vault is now 100% visual. Just drag and drop items in the sidebar to mutate your Markdown files automatically. The system obeys strict semantic laws: Note on Note ➔ Stitch: Dragging an individual marginalia over another creates a semantic link (Block ID connection) between them. Untagged on Any Box ➔ Assimilation: Drag an unclassified note into a box, and it will automatically adopt that box's tag. Minor on Major ➔ Phagocytosis (Absorption): Drag a Simple Box (e.g., #concept) into a Major Box (e.g., #research). The smaller box is absorbed and perfectly renamed to #research/concept. Equal on Equal ➔ Fusion: Drag a Simple box over another Simple box, or a Major over a Major. The system will prompt you for a new name to create a brand new Super-Category wrapping both of them (e.g., merging #ideas and #tasks into #active-project). (Note: Dragging a Major over a Simple also triggers a Fusion to prevent chronological paradoxes). # ⏪ 4. The "Ghost" Undo Engine (Ctrl+Shift+Z) Experiment without fear! Because dragging and dropping physically mutates the text in your Markdown files, we built a safety net. Did you drop a box in the wrong place? Grouped the wrong notes? Just press Ctrl+Shift+Z (Undo Last Action). The system keeps a lightweight "ghost" of your last action in RAM and will instantly revert the complex fractal merge, restoring your Markdown to its exact previous state without any lag or I/O blocking. # 📤 5. Frictionless Exports (Drag-to-Note & Canvas) You can now grab an entire semantic tree (a box containing notes and sub-categories) and drag it directly into your Obsidian editor, Pinboard, or Excalidraw Canvas. Smart Markdown Assembly: It translates the box hierarchy into perfectly formatted Headings (#, ##) and bullet points. Auto-Purifier: A new setting allows you to automatically strip structural #tags and Obsidian ^block-ids from the exported text, ensuring your final document is clean and ready to publish. Customizable Templates: Go to Settings to define exactly how dragged notes are formatted using variables like {{text}}, {{source_note}}, {{citation}}, and {{time}}. # 🎨 6. Structural Colors You can now click the new 🎨 Palette icon appearing when you hover over a box's header. Pick a color, and the entire structural box will be painted. This color is saved exclusively for the box structure without altering the original colors of your individual marginaliae. Time to organize your mind. Happy stitching and mapping! 🧠✨
releases.name: v4.6.7 - The Semantic Threads Engine 🕸️

releases.name: v4.6.5 - Hotfix: Reading Mode & Scroll

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
✨ Core & Active Recall Improvements

    Encapsulated Syntax: Time Machine links and block identifiers (^id) are now cleanly and safely hidden inside the %% tags (e.g., Answer %%> question;; [[Link]] ^id %%).

    Full Backward Compatibility: The engine can now seamlessly read both the new and old syntax. None of your previous threads or flashcards will be lost!

    Active Recall (Blur) Fixed: Resolved an issue where the blur effect wouldn't apply correctly if a flashcard contained links or IDs after the ;;.

🧠 Anki Sync Addon

    Sync Restored: The Anki engine now correctly parses the new syntax, cleanly separating questions from answers without breaking Rhizome's semantic connections.

🌱 Rhizome Time Machine & Margidoro

    Precise Stitching: Connecting notes directly from the interactive canvas now safely injects links inside the tags, preventing text clutter in Reading View.

    Dynamic Timeline: Rhizome now sorts cards by modification date rather than creation date. Old notes you edit or review today will accurately appear in today's column.

    Anti-Logs Filter: Daily reports generated by the Pomodoro timer (Margidoro) are now automatically hidden from the Rhizome timeline to keep your canvas clean.

📊 Dashboard & Spaced Repetition (SRS)

    Study Progress Secured: Fixed a critical bug in the deck builder (buildDeck) that failed to read IDs under the new syntax. This prevents the 1-3-7 day spaced repetition algorithm from accidentally resetting your card progress.
releases.name: v4.6.4 - Flashcard Syntax Overhaul & Stability Fixes

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description: We’ve added a highly requested feature for users who prefer maximum clarity when editing raw Markdown! By default, Obsidian’s CodeMirror engine renders Cornell Marginalia blocks even in Source Mode. With this update, you now have the power to bypass the visual rendering and see your notes exactly as they are written (%%> your text %%). What's new: Source Mode Toggle: A new option has been added to the settings. When enabled, switching Obsidian to Source Mode will reveal the pure, raw Markdown syntax of your marginalias instead of the rendered UI boxes. Seamless transition: The change applies instantly as you toggle between Live Preview and Source Mode, providing a distraction-free editing experience for power users. ⚙️ How to use it: Go to Settings > Cornell Marginalia. Scroll down to the Advanced & Exclusions section. Turn on the "Show Syntax in Source Mode" toggle.
releases.name: v4.6.3 - Raw Syntax Visibility in Source Mode

**Fixes:**
* **Smart Review Modal:** Fixed an issue where old marginalias appeared in the end-of-session review. It now correctly takes a snapshot and only shows *new or edited* notes from your current Pomodoro.

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description: Welcome to the biggest update of Cornell Marginalia yet! We are thrilled to introduce the Ultimate Study Dashboard—a centralized, highly customizable, and interactive command center designed to take your study sessions, spaced repetition (SRS), and time management to the next level. With i3wm-inspired multi-workspaces, seamless third-party integrations, and dynamic gamification, studying inside Obsidian has never been this powerful. ⚙️ How to Activate & Prerequisites To get the most out of the Dashboard, make sure you have the right modules enabled in your settings: Enable the Dashboard Addon: Go to Settings > Cornell Marginalia > Addons and toggle on the Cornell Dashboard 🚀. Enable Margidoro (Highly Recommended): The dashboard relies on the Margidoro 🍅 addon to track your study time and generate the new visual heatmaps. Make sure it's activated! Open the Dashboard: Click the new "layout-dashboard" icon in your left-hand ribbon menu, or use the Command Palette (Ctrl/Cmd + P) and search for Open Cornell Dashboard. (Optional but powerful) TaskNotes Integration: If you use the [TaskNotes](https://github.com/callumalpass/tasknotes) plugin, go to its settings and enable the HTTP API. Our dashboard will dynamically detect your port and sync your tasks automatically! 🌟 Core Features & Widgets 🗂️ Multi-Workspace Layout Engine Say goodbye to rigid layouts. Our new grid engine allows you to drag, drop, and toggle widgets. Create multiple "Workspaces" (e.g., one for Morning Planning, one for Deep Study) and switch between them instantly using the top navigation bar. ⏳ The Interactive Exam Timeline A spatial, horizontal timeline that visualizes your upcoming exams and tasks: GitHub-Style Study Heatmap: The timeline axis now features a beautiful, color-coded heatmap based on your Margidoro study sessions. See your consistency at a glance! Dynamic Mastery Colors: Exam names on the timeline glow in different colors (from red to cyan) based on your real-time Spaced Repetition mastery and confidence scores. Click on an exam to instantly open its project note. TaskNotes Diamonds: Upcoming tasks appear as interactive diamonds. If multiple tasks fall on the same day, they elegantly stack—hover over them to see them fan out like a deck of cards! 📅 Widget: Daily Planner Your day, optimized. Choose between Optimal, Regular, Survival, or Custom energy modes to load different routine blocks. Auto-SRS Blocks: Automatically injects study blocks if you have overdue flashcards for a specific subject. TaskNotes Sync: Tasks due today seamlessly populate into your timeline. They even support variable durations and nest themselves visually (with dashed lines and smaller icons) under your main study blocks. 📚 Widget: Subjects & Resources The heart of your knowledge base. Group your PDFs, Canvas, Excalidraw, and Markdown files by subject. See exactly how many flashcards you have and your current Mastery percentage. Syllabus Manager: Break down your subject into topics and track how many notes match your tags. Auto-Project Generation: With TaskNotes enabled, adding topics to your syllabus automatically creates a Project Note in Obsidian and pushes the topics as actionable tasks (with #cornell tags and contexts) straight to your task manager. Study & Cram Modes: Launch holistic reading sessions or strict flashcard reviews with a single click. 📊 Widget: Daily Tracker & Review Korean-Style 24h Grid: Visualizes exactly which 10-minute blocks of the day you spent studying (powered by Margidoro). Needs Review Board: Displays marginalia and flashcards that require your immediate attention. Pin them to the Explorer board or mark them as mastered directly from the widget. 📓 Widget: Daily Note Integration (Hybrid) Read, write, and interact with your native Obsidian Daily Note without leaving the dashboard. It features quick-capture inputs and auto-updates if you edit the note in another pane. 💡 Pro Tip for TaskNotes Users While in the Dashboard's "Edit Mode" (the layout icon in the top right), you can toggle the TaskNotes integration individually for the Planner, the Subjects widget, and the Timeline. You can even filter the Timeline to show only tasks generated by Cornell Marginalia!
releases.name: v4.6.0 - 🚀 Release: The Ultimate Study Dashboard

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
✨ New Main Features
🧠 Advanced Anki Synchronization (PDF++ support)

We have built a native synchronization engine that connects your Obsidian notes directly to Anki via AnkiConnect.

    Native PDF++ Rendering:  The plugin now intercepts your PDF++ embeds (![[File.pdf#page=1&rect=...]]), opens the binary file in memory, renders the page, and crops the image with millimeter precision to send it directly to Anki. All of this happens in the background, without touching your original Markdown.

    Vault Bulk Sync (Tag Mappings): Added a new "Anki Auto-Sync (Tag Mappings)" panel in the settings. You can now link specific Obsidian tags (e.g., #medicine) to specific Anki decks. With a single command, the plugin will scan your entire vault and send only the flashcards that are ready and tagged.

    Bidirectional Sync: When sending a card, the plugin injects an invisible identifier (^anki-ID) into your marginalia. If you edit the card in Obsidian and sync again, the card will be updated in Anki instead of duplicated.

🙈 Spoiler Prevention: Blur Explanatory Marginalias

    Active Recall Anti-Spoiler Filter: Introduced a new settings option. If you have regular explanatory marginalias sharing the same line or block as an active recall flashcard (;;), the plugin will automatically apply a blur effect over the explanation. This prevents you from accidentally reading the answer while trying to recall the core concept.
releases.name: v4.5.9 - The Active Recall & Anki Sync Update

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
# 🚀 New Features
## Custom Templating Engine
* **🎨 Custom Templating Engine**: You can now define your own Markdown templates for exports. Head over to **Settings > File & Output Management** to set custom paths for:
  * **Zettelkasten Notes** (`zkTemplatePath`)
  * **Pinboard Headers** (`pinboardTemplatePath`)
  * **Pinboard Individual Items** (`pinboardItemTemplatePath`)
  * **Canvas/Evidence Board Cards** (`canvasItemTemplatePath`)
* **🧠 Smart Variables**: Inside your templates, you can now use dynamic variables like `{{title}}`, `{{date}}`, `{{time}}`, `{{text}}`, `{{source_note}}`, and `{{citation}}`. 

### 🤖 Intelligent Context & Source Detection

* **The Citation Hunter (`{{citation}}`)**: When exporting boards, the plugin now automatically scans the original document. If it finds a blockquote (like a PDF highlight) or a context line immediately below your marginalia, it captures it! Your exported boards will no longer have "orphan" ideas.
* **Smart Zettelkasten Origins (`{{source_note}}`)**: When using the OmniCapture ZK mode, the plugin reads which file you are currently viewing and automatically links your new atomic note back to that source.
* **Pinboard Dominant Source**: When compiling a Pinboard, the plugin calculates a "popularity contest" among all your pinned notes to intelligently deduce the main source material, injecting it into your board's header.

### 🔗 Integrations & Under the Hood

* **🌳 Enhanced Excalidraw Mindmaps**: The "Copy to Mindmap" feature has been upgraded. It maintains the strict Markdown list formatting required by Excalidraw (`- [[Link]] \n \t - ![[image]]`) but now injects the original context/citation as sub-nodes for richer mindmaps.
* **📐 Canvas Geometry Preserved**: The Evidence Board exporter has been refactored. It now perfectly wraps your custom Markdown templates inside the main node while preserving the visual hierarchy, node colors, and the context-branching (`edges`) that makes the Canvas so useful.
* **🧹 Clean Anchors**: The contextual extraction now automatically cleans up raw Obsidian block identifiers (`^id`) to keep your exported canvases and boards looking pristine.

### 📖 Documentation
A new section has been added to the README explaining how to create templates and use variables. Feel free to copy our ready-to-use examples to get started instantly!

## Super Doodle now reads your handwriting! (OCR Update) 🔤

Visual note-taking just leveled up in Cornell Marginalia! This update brings a massive new feature directly into the Super Doodle canvas: Optical Character Recognition (OCR) powered by Tesseract.js.

🔤 From Doodle to Text: Transform your quick handwritten notes into editable digital text with a single click. Perfect for cleaning up diagrams or rapid scribbles at lightning speed.

    Frictionless Workflow: 1. Draw on the canvas.
    2. Use the Lasso tool to isolate a specific word, or just let the plugin analyze the entire canvas automatically.
    3. Click the new green "🔤 OCR" button.
    4. Your strokes will instantly turn into a floating text box. Fix any misread letters, press Enter, and the text will be permanently stamped onto the canvas (matching your currently selected color).
    5. Bonus: The recognized text is automatically copied to your clipboard.

    Bilingual Support: Natively optimized to recognize both English and Spanish characters.

🛠️ Geek Info (Under the Hood)

    Auto-Image Optimization: To guarantee the highest possible accuracy, Super Doodle isolates your strokes, scales the selection up by 200%, and applies an extreme high-contrast filter in the background before sending it to the OCR engine.

    Anti-Chaos Filter (Whitelist): The engine is strictly limited to common letters, numbers, and punctuation marks. This prevents the AI from hallucinating weird symbols (@, #, ^) if your handwriting gets a little messy.

    Lazy Loading: Tesseract.js won't bloat your Obsidian startup time. The script is dynamically downloaded from the CDN only the very first time you hit the OCR button during a session.
releases.name: v4.5.8 - The Custom Templates Update

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
### ✨ What's New

🎨 **Smart Auto-Crop for Super Doodle**
Creating "Massive" or "Insane" sized canvases in the Super Doodle Addon is now 100% efficient. 
* **The Problem:** Previously, saving a large canvas would save the entire bounding box, resulting in huge file sizes and massive blank spaces in your Markdown notes.
* **The Solution:** We've built a new pixel-scanning engine that acts as an intelligent guillotine. When you save a Doodle, it automatically detects your actual strokes, crops out all the empty transparent space, and saves only what matters.
* **The Result:** Drastically reduced attachment file sizes, faster loading times, and perfectly sized images inside your notes and Evidence Boards.

🔍 **Interactive Lightbox (Zoom & Pan) for Rhizome**
Viewing your Doodles in full-screen just got a major upgrade. Clicking the "Maximize" button on a visual note (inside the Rhizome Time Machine or the Explorer) now opens a brand-new Interactive Lightbox.
* **Zoom:** Use your mouse wheel to smoothly zoom in and out of your high-resolution doodles.
* **Pan:** Click and drag the image to freely pan around your drawing, just like navigating a professional infinite canvas (Miro/Figma style).
* **Smart Theming:** Doodles automatically adapt to Dark Mode (smart color inversion) and feature a clean, white background pad for optimal visibility.

### 🛠️ Under the Hood Improvements
* Improved memory cleanup during canvas destruction to prevent memory leaks during long study sessions.
* Restored native hover tooltips behavior when dragging and dropping visual notes.
releases.name: v4.5.7 - Super Doodle Auto-Crop & Interactive Lightbox!

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
✨ New Features

    🎚️ Margin Distance (Offset) Control: Added a new slider in the settings! You now have full manual control over how close or far the marginalia sits from your main text. This is a game-changer for users with varying monitor sizes or specific "Readable Line Length" preferences.

    📐 Symmetrical Inverse Alignment: The offset dynamically applies to both standard (%%>%%) and inverse (%%<%%) marginalia, ensuring perfect symmetry across your document.

🐛 Bug Fixes & Improvements

    📖 Reading View Stability: Fixed a critical bug where absolute positioning and CSS specificity clashes would cause marginalia to overlap or break out of its column during Reading Mode.

    🛠️ Live Preview Stacking: Resolved alignment conflicts where inverse marginalia would inherit incorrect float properties, ensuring smooth stacking.

    ⚡ Performance Boost: Moved dynamic layout calculations to a centralized "Master Variable" approach within the JavaScript engine. This offloads the heavy lifting to the browser's CSS engine (GPU), preventing layout thrashing and keeping the editor running at a smooth 60 FPS.

How to use the new offset:
Go to Settings > Cornell Marginalia > Appearance & Rendering and adjust the Margin Distance (Offset) slider until your notes look exactly how you want them!
releases.name: v4.5.6 - The Alignment & Offset Update

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description: We heard your feedback! Many of you requested a way to keep marginalia readable even when opening sidebars or working on narrower screens. This update introduces a brand new, highly optimized Responsive Mode that automatically collapses marginal notes into the main text body when space gets tight. ✨ New Features Auto-Collapsing Marginalia (Opt-in): Marginalia will now intelligently flow inside the text block if your note pane becomes too narrow. Custom Responsive Threshold: You are in full control. A new slider in the settings allows you to set the exact pixel width (from 400px to 1200px) at which the marginalia should collapse. ⚙️ How to Enable Because we want to preserve the classic Cornell Marginalia experience for existing users, this feature is disabled by default. To turn it on: Go to Settings > Cornell Marginalia. Toggle on Responsive Marginalia (Auto-Collapse). Adjust the Responsive Threshold (px) slider to fit your personal workflow and screen size. ⚠️ Note on Reading Mode: Due to how Obsidian renders hidden HTML blocks in Reading Mode, this responsive feature is strictly designed for Live Preview and Source Mode to prevent massive empty spaces in your notes.
releases.name: v4.5.5 - The Responsive Update 📱

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
Get ready to supercharge your study sessions! This massive update transforms Cornell Marginalia from a simple note-taking tool into a complete, knowledge-aware study engine. We've introduced powerful active recall features, a smart Pomodoro timer, and squashed some major bugs.

⚠️ Important Note: Opt-in Features!
To keep your workspace lightweight and distraction-free, all new major addons are disabled by default. To start using them, head over to Settings > Cornell Marginalia > Addons & Modules and toggle them on!

🌟 New Features
🧠 Blurting Mode (Active Recall Engine)

Test your memory and consolidate your knowledge with the new Blurting Addon! Based on the proven "Blurting" study method and Spaced Repetition (1-3-7 intervals).

    Visual Blurting (Zen Doodle): Draw everything you remember from your flashcards on a blank canvas, then enter the "Audit Phase" (Red Pen Mode) to correct your mistakes side-by-side with your original notes.

    Textual Blurting: Prefer typing? Generate a fresh markdown note to write down your thoughts and compare them later.

    Built-in Spaced Repetition: The plugin automatically schedules your next review (Day 1, 3, 7, and Mastered) based on your performance. Check the new Reviews tab in the Explorer!

🍅 Margidoro Engine (Knowledge-Aware Pomodoro)

More than just a timer, Margidoro tracks what you actually learn during your focus blocks.

    Session Objectives: Set a goal before starting your timer to stay focused.

    Flexible Controls: Need more time? Use the new +5m button on the fly, or hit ⏭ Skip to instantly finish the current block directly from the status bar.

    Auto-Harvesting: When the timer ends, Margidoro automatically scans your vault and gathers all the marginalias you created during that specific session.

    Review & Grade: Grade your new notes as ✅ Easy, 🤔 Review, or ❌ Hard right from the end-of-session modal.

    Auto-Tagging: Start a marginalia with ? (configurable) and Margidoro will automatically pre-mark it as "Hard" for you!

    Daily Logs: Generates a beautiful Markdown summary of your session, your objectives, and your graded notes.

    Smart Reminders: Set a daily time (e.g., 20:00) in settings. A background daemon will softly notify you if you have pending "Hard" concepts to review.

🌌 Rhizome x Margidoro Integration

Your Time Machine just got smarter.

    Added a new 🍅 Pomodoro Review toggle in the Rhizome view.

    Clicking it filters the entire universe to show only the notes you marked as "Hard" or "Review" in your Pomodoro sessions.

    Mastery System: Hard notes now display a green ✅ button in the Rhizome. Click it once you've learned the concept to mark it as Mastered and clear it from your backlog!

🐛 Bug Fixes & Improvements

    X-Ray Vision (Hover Tooltips) Fix for PDF++: * Fixed a critical issue where hovering over marginalias linked to PDFs would fail to crop the exact page/selection. The tooltip engine now perfectly strips markdown aliases (e.g., |Alias) before sending the link to PDF++, restoring seamless integration.

        Resolved an issue where dragging cards in the Rhizome 3D/2D views would trigger ghostly hover tooltips or throw wasDragged console errors.

    OmniCapture Stability: * Improved the OmniCapture logic to prevent "ghost notes" tracking issues.

        Under the hood: Removed forced global Block ID injection from the standard capture tool. The plugin is now smarter and only injects Block IDs silently when explicitly required by the Spaced Repetition or Pomodoro engines, keeping your raw markdown cleaner.

    UI Polish: Cleaned up duplicated UI elements in the sidebar and optimized the spacing for action buttons inside the Rhizome cards.
releases.name: v4.5.4 - The Focus & Active Recall Update 🧠🍅

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
### ✨ What's New
* **Adaptive Width Mode:** A new toggle in settings that intelligently calculates the exact empty space on your screen and resizes the marginalia dynamically. No more overlapping text!

### 🧠 Under the Hood (For the curious)
Instead of relying on fixed percentages (`25%`), the new Adaptive Mode uses CSS math to check your viewport width (`vw`) against Obsidian's native `--file-line-width` variable. By wrapping this in a `clamp()` function, the plugin ensures your notes always stay within a readable size without invading your main text area.

### ⚙️ How to use it
1. Go to **Settings** > **Cornell Marginalia**.
2. Scroll to the **Appearance & Rendering** section.
3. Turn ON **Adaptive Width (Theme Compatibility)**.
releases.name: v4.5.2 - Adaptive Width for Custom Themes

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
This update fixes the visual limitations of the Editorial Blocks and improves context visibility.
✨ Key Improvements

    🔓 Live Preview Block Fix: Marginalia notes inside ```cornell blocks are no longer clipped or hidden when the cursor is outside the block. They now float freely in the margins as intended.

    🧠 Full-Context Hover: Fixed the hover tooltips to show the entire paragraph instead of a truncated 3-line slice. This also applies to the context notes in Evidence Boards (Canvas).

    🧹 Clean Previews: Code block markers and internal block IDs are now automatically hidden in hover and canvas previews for a cleaner look.
releases.name: v4.5.1 - Live Preview & Hover Fixes

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description: The core philosophy of Cornell Marginalia has always been to maintain a fluid, organic, and frictionless writing flow. From day one, the goal was to avoid relying on heavy "code blocks" (````) that interrupt your focus or clutter your raw Markdown. However, as our notes grow in complexity, there are specific scenarios where strict structural precision and perfect alignment between a paragraph and its marginalia become absolutely critical. For those users who require millimeter-perfect layout control in their complex compositions, today we are introducing the Cornell Editorial Block. ✨ What's New? 📦 The cornell Block: By wrapping your text in a ````cornell` block, you ensure that the marginal note and the main text align flawlessly, regardless of complex line breaks, images, or nested lists. 🖨️ Full PDF Print Support: The architecture of this block has been specifically designed to survive Obsidian's PDF export engine. Your blocks will maintain their stretched column structure (Stretch Flexbox) when printing, and cleanly restore to pure Markdown when you are done. 🔗 Intact Ecosystem: Marginalias inside the block retain 100% of their superpowers. They fully support threads, directional compasses, image injections, and colored tags just like standard inline notes. ⚡ Frictionless Workflow Knowing that manually typing the block syntax is tedious and breaks concentration, we've built in automations so you never lose your rhythm: Command Palette & Context Menu: You can now select any text, right-click (or use the command palette), and choose "Insert Cornell Block". Auto-injection & Sniper Cursor: When invoking the command, the plugin doesn't just create the block; it automatically injects the marginalia syntax (%%> %%) and magically places your cursor right in the middle, ready for you to start typing your note instantly. Usage Example: ``` ```cornell %%> Your perfectly aligned note here %% The main text that requires strict alignment goes here. ``` We want to thank the community for their continuous feedback and feature requests. We keep working hard to give you the best tools for your digital brain! ---
releases.name: v4.5.0 - The Editorial Block (Perfect Alignment)

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description: 🧭 Semantic Compass Navigation: You can now connect your notes spatially using directional metadata (e.g., [North:: [[Note]]], [South:: [[Note]]]). Even better: you don't have to type this manually! You can now assign these directions directly using the Stitch function right inside the 3D Rhizome view. This allows you to visually wire your ideas together, building a true "Directional Graph" that gives your notes physical orientation and deep semantic context with just a few clicks. 🧊 3D Rhizome Interactions: The Rhizome view is no longer flat! You can now manipulate your note cards in a fully interactive 3D space: Hold Shift + Drag/Scroll to Zoom in and out of a card. Hold Alt + Drag to Rotate cards dynamically in 3D. 🐛 Bug Fixes & Improvements 🖨️ Print/PDF Layout Alignment: Fixed an issue where marginalia (like %%>) were inverting their position (rendering on the right instead of the left) during PDF export. The printed layout now perfectly matches your live editor.
releases.name: v4.4.9 - 3D Rhizome & Semantic Compass

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
### **✨What's New**
   **⚙️ Complete Settings UI Overhaul: T**he settings tab has been completely redesigned, reorganized, and translated into English. Options are now logically grouped into clean, collapsible sections (Appearance, File Management, Addons & Modules, etc.), making it much easier to configure your marginalia experience.

**📏 Super Doodle Canvas Adjustment:** You can now adjust the canvas size directly within the Super Doodle addon, giving you more control over your drawing and brainstorming space.

### **🐛 Bug Fixes & Improvements**

**📌 Double-Save Bug Fixed:** We removed a redundant "Save" button in the Super Doodle interface that was causing doodles to be saved twice into the Pinboard. Everything saves cleanly with a single click now
releases.name: v 4.4.8 - Settings Revamp & Super Doodle Enhancements

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description: ### ✨ New Addon: Super Doodle Transform your standard Zen Doodle into a massive workstation! By activating the **Super Doodle** addon in your settings, you unlock a whole new drawing experience: * 🗺️ **Massive 4x Canvas:** A drawing area four times larger than the standard view, giving you all the space you need for complex mind maps or sketches. * 🖐️ **Pan & Navigate:** Use the new Hand tool to easily drag and navigate around your giant canvas. * 🖌️ **New Toolset:** Adjustable brush size slider and a 4-color palette (Black, Red, Blue, Green) to bring your ideas to life. * ⚡ **Direct Omni-Capture Integration:** Reading a PDF or a note? You don't need to send your drawing to the Board anymore. With the new `⚡ Omni-Capture` button, you can instantly save your drawing directly to your Inbox destination, perfectly pairing your visual thoughts with your current reading context!
releases.name: v4.4.7 - New Addon: The Super Doodle Update 🎨

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description: This release brings a major architectural fix to the PDF export rendering (prepareForPrint command), ensuring your marginalia looks as perfect on paper as it does in your vault. # ✨ What's New & Fixed: Perfect Vertical Alignment: Fixed a structural issue where marginal notes would render with a vertical offset below the text. Notes now align exactly with their corresponding paragraphs in the PDF. Restored Directionality: Left (%%<) and Right (%%>) marginalia now accurately render on their respective sides of the page during print mode. Under the Hood: Refactored the print injection logic to process text block-by-block, introducing robust data-direction attributes for the CSS print media query without breaking the restoreFromPrint functionality. # Remember: ⚠️ 10. 🖨️ PDF Export & Printing Support Obsidian's native PDF export engine notoriously struggles with floating margin notes. To solve this, Cornell Marginalia includes a dedicated "Print Engine" to guarantee your summaries look perfect and professional on paper or tablets. How to export your notes to PDF: Prepare: Open the Command Palette (Ctrl/Cmd + P) and run "Prepare Marginalia for PDF Print". Your %%> notes will temporarily transform into safe HTML tags. Export: Use Obsidian's native "Export to PDF" feature. Your marginalia will automatically align in a clean, classic left-column Cornell layout, and the main text will wrap perfectly around them. Restore: Once your PDF is saved, run "Restore Marginalia after PDF Print" to instantly return your text to its original, clean Markdown state. ⚠️ Important: Always remember to run the Restore command after exporting to keep your Markdown files clean and future-proof!
releases.name: v4.4.6 - PDF Export Overhaul

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description: ## 🎨 New Addon: PDF Doodle & Harvest Bring your visual marginalia directly to your PDFs! We've completely redesigned the capture experience on PDF documents to make it fluid, native, and 100% compatible with your favorite workflow tools. ### ✨ Key Features * **Integrated Drawing Mode:** Run the command (Cornell: Start Drawing in PDF) to inject a transparent canvas over your PDF. Draw your ideas, sketch, or point out important sections directly with your mouse or stylus. * **Rest Mode (Double Click):** Freeze your drawing instantly by double-clicking. The canvas will return cursor control to you, allowing you to freely select and copy text from the PDF underneath. * **One-Click Harvest (⚡):** Click the floating button and the plugin will handle the rest: * Automatically reads your clipboard. * Converts your doodle into a real `.png` file and saves it to your vault. * Assembles the marginalia using the exact required strict syntax: `Copied text %%> img:[[doodle.png]] %%`. * **Seamless PDF++ Integration:** Do you use the PDF++ auto-copy feature? Excellent! The Addon will read the magic link PDF++ left in your clipboard and seamlessly merge it with your doodle without any extra steps.
releases.name: v4.4.5 -🎨 PDF Doodle & Smart Harvest (Native Integration with PDF++)

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description: New: Native images and LaTeX formulas inside flashcards can now be blurred! They safely fade into the study fog without breaking your layout. Fixes: We redesigned the CSS architecture for Active Recall mode to handle highly specific themes (I love you, Velocity)
releases.name: v4.4.4 - Image Blurring & Active Recall Update

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description: ## ✨ New Features * **Rhizome to Pinboard Drag & Drop:** Fully implemented and stabilized cross-module Drag & Drop! You can now drag notes directly from the Rhizome Time Machine and drop them into your active Pinboard. * **New Margin Doodle Command:** Added a dedicated command to quickly open the drawing canvas and insert doodles directly into your notes, streamlining the visual thinking process without needing the full Omni-Capture modal. ## 🛠️ Improvements & Refactoring * **Omni-Capture Engine Refactor:** Completely restructured the Omni-Capture core logic for better performance, decoupling, and maintainability. * **Dark Mode Doodles Enhancement:** Hand-drawn doodles now feature smart color inversion and background handling in the Marginalia Explorer, making them look gorgeous and perfectly legible when using Obsidian's Dark Mode.
releases.name: v4.4.3 - Rhizome Time Machine & PinBoard Synergy Update

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
This update focuses on crucial under-the-hood performance improvements and highly requested quality-of-life features for the Rhizome Time Machine.
✨ What's New

    🧹 Automatic Garbage Collector (Memory Optimization): Previously, if you deleted a note that had Spaced Repetition (SRS) data, its ghost data would remain in your data.json file forever, eventually slowing down the plugin. Now, an automatic Garbage Collector runs silently in the background when you open the Time Machine. It scans your vault, detects orphaned data from deleted files, and cleans it up. Your plugin will now remain lightning-fast and lightweight forever.

    🔍 Cinematic Doodle Lightbox: Handwritten notes and doodles are meant to be seen in detail! Cards containing images in the Time Machine now feature a new "Expand" (Zoom) button. Clicking it opens a beautiful, full-screen cinematic Lightbox, allowing you to study your doodles and diagrams without leaving the timeline.

    🌗 Smart Dark Mode Inversion: The new Lightbox natively supports Dark Mode, perfectly inverting your handwritten black-ink doodles (doodle_...) so they glow beautifully against the dark background
releases.name: v4.4.2 - Memory Optimization & Doodle Lightbox 🧹🔍

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description: Descripción de la Release: "A rhizome has no beginning or end; it is always in the middle, between things, interbeing, intermezzo. The tree is filiation, but the rhizome is alliance, uniquely alliance. The tree imposes the verb 'to be', but the fabric of the rhizome is the conjunction, 'and... and... and...'." — Gilles Deleuze & Félix Guattari, A Thousand Plateaus We are thrilled to announce the biggest update to Cornell Marginalia yet. This release transforms the plugin from a simple margin-note tool into a full-fledged, non-linear thinking environment and memory gym. By extracting your marginalia from the confines of individual files, we've built a Time Machine that lays out your thoughts chronologically, and a Semantic Stitching engine that connects them rhizomatically—proving that knowledge isn't a timeline you follow, but a web you weave. ✨ Major New Features 🕰️ The Rhizome Time Machine (Infinite Canvas): A beautiful, infinite horizontal canvas that scans your entire vault and displays all your marginalia chronologically. Zoom in and out smoothly to see the "Big Picture" of your thoughts across days, months, or years. 🕸️ Semantic Stitching & Hover Radar: Connect notes across time! Hovering over any card reveals glowing SVG threads connecting it to other notes. You can now "Stitch" (link) two notes directly from the canvas interface without opening the files. 🎯 Quantum Focus Mode: Click the Focus (Target) icon on any note to collapse time. All unrelated notes and empty days vanish, bringing years of separated, linked thoughts side-by-side into a single, focused semantic cluster. 🧠 Spaced Repetition & Heatmap (Study Mode): Turn your timeline into an Anki-like memory gym. Toggle the "Heatmap" to color-code your notes based on memory retention (Red = New/Due, Green = Memorized). Uses a built-in SM-2 algorithm to schedule your reviews via Hard/Good/Easy buttons. ⚡ Flashcard Filtering & Fast Search: A new top bar allows for instantaneous, RAM-cached searching. Filter by your custom color tags, or isolate only your flashcards (notes ending in ;;) for a distraction-free study session. 🌌 Ultimate Aesthetic Customization: Total control over your environment. Add a custom wallpaper URL (image or GIF), and adjust the opacity and blur sliders to achieve a gorgeous, glassmorphism UI where your notes float like holograms over your favorite aesthetic background. Native support for inverted colors on handwritten doodles in dark mode! 🛠️ How to install / update Download the main.js, styles.css, and manifest.json files attached below. Replace your existing plugin files in .obsidian/plugins/your-plugin-folder/. Reload Obsidian. Open the plugin settings, enable the "Time Machine & Rhizome" module, and click the new branching icon on your left ribbon to enter the canvas! Happy thinking, and enjoy weaving your rhizome! 🌱
releases.name: v4.4.1 - The Rhizome Update: Time Machine, Semantic Stitching & Spaced Repetition 🌱

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description: This update introduces a major architectural leap for Cornell Marginalia: The Addons & Modules System. We know that Obsidian users love to customize their workspaces, but we also know that many prefer a strict, distraction-free environment. That is why we have rebuilt the plugin to support a modular architecture. **Everything new in this update is 100% OPTIONAL and disabled by default**. If you love the clean, core experience, you don't have to change a thing. But if you want to spice up your Marginalia Explorer, you now have the power to do so! 🧩 New: Addons System You will now find a new "Addons & Modules" section at the bottom of the plugin settings. Here, you can toggle specific features on or off in real-time without bloating your core experience. 🎮 Addon: Gamification & User Profile Turn your note-taking into a rewarding habit! Level Up: Earn 10 XP for every marginalia you capture via the Omni-Capture or the sidebar. Custom Avatar: Paste an image URL to set up your own profile picture in the Explorer sidebar. Inspirational Quote: Add a custom bio or quote right below your XP bar to keep you motivated. 🖼️ Addon: Custom Explorer Background Give your Marginalia Explorer a personalized, aesthetic look. Custom Background Image: Add any image URL to use as the background of your sidebar. Lo-Fi Blur Effect: Adjust the blur slider to soften the background image, creating a beautiful glassmorphism effect that keeps your text readable. Dark Overlay: Fine-tune the background opacity to darken the image so your marginalia colors still pop perfectly. 🛠️ Under the Hood Complete refactoring to support isolated TypeScript modules. Added an internal caching layer for module states.

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
Description:
This update represents one of the biggest architectural leaps for the plugin. We've rewritten the scanning engine to achieve instant speeds, added deep integration with PDFs (Zotlike Mode), unified the drawing experience, and polished dozens of UI details for a frictionless workflow.

🚀 Features

    📚 Zotlike Mode (Smart PDF Context): The Current tab now automatically detects if you're reading a PDF. Instead of failing, it scans the entire vault at lightning speed and shows you in the sidebar all the marginalias from your Zettelkasten that mention or link to that exact document, accompanied by a sleek new persistent Banner.

    ✏️ Editable Pinboard Nodes: Finally! You can now double-click any title (#) or text node inside the Board (Pinboard) to edit it directly in the list, and press Enter to save it without losing the original formatting.

⚡ Performance

    🧠 RAM Vault Cache (Extreme Speed): No more lag when scanning hundreds of notes. We've implemented an mtime (modified time) based caching engine. If a note hasn't changed, the plugin reads from RAM in milliseconds instead of processing heavy Regex.

    🖼️ Image Path Cache: Optimized the search for visual resources to avoid unnecessary hard drive reads and speed up the sidebar rendering.

🎨 UI & UX

    📸 True Thumbnails in Explorer: Image marginalias (Doodles or snippets) are now rendered as elegant 35px thumbnails directly in the sidebar list, replacing the old "🖼️ [Image]" text placeholder.

    🎯 Invisible Drop Zone: Added an invisible 60px drop zone at the bottom of the Board. Dragging and dropping notes to the very end of a long list is now natural and effortless.

    🖌️ The Zen Doodle mode  now includes the native Eraser tool and an optimized attach button.

    🖌️ Complete cleanup of the button bars in the Omni-Capture and Margin Doodle modals.⚡ Save (Zap) button was configured to save and inject the note instantly (supports Ctrl+Enter shortcut)
releases.name: v4.3.9 -  📚 📚The Zotlike  📚📚🚀

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
## What's New: The "Quality of Life" Update 🚀

This update focuses purely on polishing the user experience, specially for users who prefer a mouse-driven workflow.

### ✨ New Features
* **Native Context Menu:** Added new options to the right-click menu. You can now select text and right-click to instantly **Insert a Margin Note**, open the **Omni-Capture**, or **Draw a Doodle**, completely bypassing the command palette.
* **Custom Inbox Folder:** Added a new setting (`Omni-Capture Default Folder`). You can now designate a specific folder for new notes created via Omni-Capture, keeping your vault root perfectly clean.

### 🛠️ Improvements & Fixes
* **Sidebar Omni-Capture Sync:** The sidebar's quick capture now fully respects the new default folder settings and ZK mode parameters, aligning its behavior with the main Omni-Capture modal.
* General code cleanup and UI stabilization.
releases.name: v4.3.8 - Native Context Menu

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
## ✨ New Features
* 🎨 **Zen Doodle Mode:** Introducing a new full-panel drawing canvas in the Explorer. You can now enter a distraction-free mode to create complex sketches and freehand diagrams, and instantly attach them as visual nodes to your Pinboard or Omni-Capture.

## 🐛 Bug Fixes & Improvements
* 🖨️ **PDF Export Engine (Complete Overhaul):**
  * **Stacking Fix (No more overlapping):** Abandoned `position: absolute` in the print stylesheet in favor of a `float` and `display: flow-root` architecture. Now, multiple marginalias within the same paragraph will stack fluidly downwards without overlapping or breaking the document flow.
  * **Native Image Support in PDFs:** Fixed the issue where the `img: [[...]]` syntax was printed as raw text. The engine now dynamically resolves local absolute paths (`app://local/...`), allowing your doodles and captures to render natively in the final PDF.
  * **Thread Link Cleanup:** The print preparation engine now intelligently strips internal thread connections (e.g., `[[Note > ^id]]`) so your exported PDF remains clean and readable, keeping only the relevant margin note.
  * **Left-Alignment Support:** The pre-print preparation script now correctly detects, parses, and formats reverse-direction marginalia (`%%<`).
* 🛠️ **TypeScript Stability:** Resolved strict typing issues when processing regular expression arrays (`RegExpMatchArray`) to ensure safe and reliable compilation.

***
**Developer Note:** For the best results when exporting to PDF from Obsidian, remember to run the `"Prepare Marginalia for PDF Print"` command before exporting, and `"Restore Marginalia after PDF Print"` once you are done!
releases.name: v4.3.7 - Zen Doodle & Flawless PDF Exports

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
🐛 Bug Fixes

    Anti-Chain Shield (ZK Shield): Fixed a critical bug where ZK mode would accumulate and merge old IDs (e.g., 2026... - 2026...). The engine now clears the memory and guarantees perfect atomic IDs on every use.

    Fixed an issue that forced Doodles to be saved in Obsidian's default attachment folder, ignoring the user's custom settings.

    Removed duplicate options in the Settings panel and resolved the TS1005 compilation error.
releases.name: v4.3.5 - ZK fix

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
### 🌟 New Features
* **Sidebar Omni-Capture:** A completely redesigned, persistent Omni-Capture bar right in the Explorer. Capture ideas, clipboard context, and doodles without opening modals.
* **Smart Zettelkasten Mode:** Toggle the `🗃️ ZK` button to automatically generate unique atomic notes (YYYYMMDDHHmmss) for your captures.
* **Folder Management:** You can now configure specific output folders in Settings for your ZK notes, Doodles, Evidence Boards, and Pinboards.
* **Smart Auto-Paste:** The Omni-Capture now intelligently ignores duplicate clipboard content to keep your notes clean.

### 🎨 UI & UX Improvements
* **Minimalist Pinboard Toolbar:** Replaced the bulky buttons in the Board tab with a sleek, native-looking icon toolbar.
* **Context-Aware UI:** Search bar and action buttons automatically hide when you are in the Board tab to maximize vertical space.
* **Keyboard Navigation:** Added new hotkeys `Alt + A` (Focus Board Input) and `Alt + C` (Focus Omni-Capture) for lightning-fast workflows.
* Improved icons across the plugin using Obsidian's native Lucide icons for a cleaner look.
releases.name: v4.3.4 - The Zettelkasten & Omni-Capture Update ⚡

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
Building a mindmap or outline should feel as natural as typing in a blank document. In this mini-update, we've supercharged the Pinboard's text input to understand native Markdown-style indentation!
🌳 Smart Hyphen Indentation

You no longer need to rely exclusively on Alt + Enter or the Arrow keys to create child nodes. You can now define the exact hierarchy of your new node directly from the "Add text" bar by simply typing hyphens (-).

How it works:
The number of hyphens dictates the indentation level. The plugin automatically cleans up the hyphens so your board looks pristine:

    Root Idea (Level 0 - Normal Enter)

    - Child idea (Level 1)

    -- Grandchild idea (Level 2)

    --- Great-grandchild idea (Level 3)

Combined with the Persistent Input Focus from our last update, you can now rapid-fire an entire complex hierarchical outline without your hands ever leaving the keyboard. Pure Flow State! ⚡
✨ Updated Placeholder

    The text input placeholder has been updated to politely remind you of your new superpowers: Add text (# for titles, - for children).
releases.name: v4.3.3 - The Rapid Outlining Syntax 📝

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
### 🚀 Release vX.X.X: The "Flow State" & Speed Update ⚡

This major update is all about **Quality of Life (QoL)**, frictionless keyboard navigation, and keeping you in the zone. We've introduced powerful new shortcuts, rapid-fire data entry, and a background clipboard listener specifically designed for heavy readers and PDF power users.

#### 🤖 Auto-Paste Engine (The PDF Reader's Best Friend)

* **New "Auto-Paste" Mode in the Board**: Reading a dense document with tools like PDF++? Click **▶ Auto OFF** to turn on the background listener. Every time you copy text to your clipboard, it will automatically appear as a new node in your Pinboard. No more switching panels or opening Omni-Capture repeatedly. Just read, copy, and watch your mindmap build itself in real-time!

#### ⚡ Rapid-Fire Outlining

* **Persistent Input Focus**: When typing manually in the Pinboard's "Add text" bar, pressing `Enter` no longer steals your focus. The cursor stays right where it is, allowing you to brain-dump multiple ideas, lists, or headers like a machine gun without ever touching your mouse.

#### ⌨️ Keyboard Mastery & Shortcuts

We've completely overhauled the Marginalia Explorer so your hands never have to leave the keyboard:

**In the Explorer (Current/Vault Tabs):**

* **Mass Pinning (`Shift + Arrows`)**: Hold `Shift` while pressing `Arrow Up/Down` to rapidly pin multiple marginalias to your board in seconds.
* **Source Jump (`Ctrl/Cmd + Enter`)**: Press this on any sidebar item to immediately open the original file and jump directly to that line.
* **Instant List Focus**: Pressing `Arrow Down` from the search bar instantly drops your focus to the first item in the results list.

**In the Pinboard (Board Tab):**

* **Contextual Insert (`Enter`)**: Pressing `Enter` while focusing on a board node instantly takes you to the text input. Type your thought, hit Enter, and it will be inserted *exactly below* the node you were on, inheriting its indentation.
* **Quick Child Node (`Alt + Enter`)**: Pressing `Alt + Enter` on a node tells the board to make your new text a *child* of that node (automatically adds 1 level of indentation). Building hierarchical mindmap trees is now blazing fast!
* **Global Input Focus (`Alt + A`)**: Press `Alt + A` to instantly jump to the "Add text" bar in the Pinboard from anywhere.

**In Omni-Capture:**

* **Quick Save (`Ctrl/Cmd + Enter`)**: Instantly save and inject your capture without having to reach for the mouse.

#### 🎨 UI Tweaks & 🐛 Bug Fixes

* **Reorganized Board Actions**: The Pinboard top menu is now cleanly organized into logical rows (`[📝 Note] [🎨 Canvas]` and `[🗑️ Clear] [▶ Auto OFF]`).
* **Non-Destructive Exports**: Exporting to a Note no longer auto-clears your board. You now have full control with the dedicated `🗑️ Clear` button when you are truly ready to wipe your workspace.
* **Bug Fix (Mass Pinning)**: Fixed a `TypeError` that occurred when using `Shift + Arrow` to mass-pin custom or virtual nodes (like titles and auto-pasted text) that didn't have an origin file. The engine is now 100% bulletproof.

---
releases.name: Release v4.3.1: The "Flow State" & Speed Update ⚡

Full Pinboard Synergy: Highlights inherit all the power of the Marginalia Board. releases.description:
✨ New Features & Workflow:

* **"Paste" Active Skeleton (Board Tab):** Added a new "📥 Paste" button to the Board. With a single click, the plugin scans your currently active note, extracts all of its headings (`#`) and bullet points (`-`), and instantly imports them as a structural skeleton onto your Board. It's the ultimate tool for quickly setting up the scaffolding of a study topic before you start pinning marginalias to it!

    Global Hotkeys (Frictionless Entry): Added global, customizable Obsidian commands. Press Alt+E to instantly open, reveal, and focus the Marginalia Explorer without touching the mouse. Press Alt+R to refresh the vault scan.

    Vim-like Navigation: The entire Explorer is now fully navigable via keyboard. Use ArrowUp/ArrowDown to seamlessly jump through your marginalias (automatically skipping over group headers).
