releases.name: v4.2.8 - The "Keyboard Supremacy" Update ⌨️ & Mass Stitching

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
### 🩹 Hotfix: Ghost Tooltip Annihilation 👻 & Omni-Capture Polish

* **Bug Fix (Ghost Tooltips):** Resolved a UI issue where hover tooltips would remain floating on the screen after an item was removed from the Pinboard or Explorer. Tooltips are now instantly destroyed upon interaction.
* **UX Polish (Smart Cache):** Refined the Omni-Capture clipboard behavior. The modal now features a "short-term memory" to ignore previously captured clipboard content, preventing the modal from feeling cluttered, and includes a manual "Clear" button.
* **Rock-Solid Stability:** Ensured 100% native rendering for Obsidian callouts (`> [!cornell]`) alongside the new capture features.
releases.name: v4.2.7 - Hotfix: Ghost Tooltip Annihilation 👻 & Omni-Capture Polish

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
### 🚀v4.2.6 - The Omni-Capture Update ⚡ & Bulletproof Stability

This major update introduces a frictionless way to capture your "Aha! moments" without ever leaving your current document or PDF, while reinforcing the plugin's core stability.

**⚡ The Omni-Capture Engine:**
* **Global Quick Capture:** Trigger the new `Omni-Capture` command (`Alt+C` recommended) from anywhere in Obsidian. A sleek, distraction-free modal will appear to capture your thoughts instantly.
* **Smart Clipboard & Image Support:** The modal automatically reads your clipboard text to use as context. You can also paste (`Ctrl+V`) screenshots or images directly into the modal, which will generate a live preview and save the image securely to your vault.
* **Integrated Doodle Canvas:** Need to sketch your idea? The Omni-Capture modal includes a hidden drawing canvas. Click "Add Doodle" to sketch with your mouse or tablet and attach it to your thought.
* **Vault Autocomplete & Memory:** The destination input now auto-suggests `.md` files from your vault and remembers your last used destination (e.g., `Marginalia Inbox.md`), ensuring a blazing-fast GTD (Getting Things Done) workflow.

**🛡️ Bulletproof Callout & Block Stability:**
* **Perfected Single-Line Injection:** We heavily optimized the injection engine. Marginalias, images, and doodles are now safely packaged into a single, indestructible line.
* **100% Reading View Compatibility:** This structural update guarantees that your invisible `> [!cornell]` grouping callouts, nested lists, and complex markdown blocks render flawlessly in Reading View without breaking Obsidian's native parser.
releases.name: v4.2.6 -  The Omni-Capture Update ⚡ & Bulletproof Stability

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
### 🚀  - The "True Marginalia" & Doodle Engine Update

This update brings the original spirit of marginalia back to life. You can now sketch, draw, and annotate directly in the margins of your digital notes, seamlessly integrated with your spatial Evidence Boards and Excalidraw Mindmaps!

**✏️ The Doodle Engine (True Marginalia):**
* **Native Drawing Canvas:** Trigger the new `Draw a Doodle` command to open a distraction-free floating canvas. Supports both mouse and stylus/drawing tablets.
* **Auto-Injection:** Clicking "Save to Margin" automatically saves your sketch as a PNG in your vault and injects the image syntax into your active note. No file management required!

**🌗 Smart Dark-Mode Ink & Aesthetics:**
* **Chameleon CSS:** Doodles are saved with a transparent background. If you switch Obsidian to Dark Mode, the plugin's CSS intelligently inverts your doodles to crisp white ink, ensuring they are always perfectly visible.


**🧠 Canvas & Excalidraw Perfection:**
* **Evidence Board Images:** The native `.canvas` export now fully parses and renders your images and doodles directly inside the Canvas cards, rather than displaying placeholder text.
* **Excalidraw Node Cleanup:** The `📋 Clip` Mindmap exporter is now completely image-aware. 
  * If a marginalia has text *and* a doodle, it creates a parent node (text) with a child node (image).
  * If a marginalia is *purely* a doodle, it exports as a clean, stand-alone image node without any ghost text or broken links.
releases.name: v4.2.5 - The "True Marginalia" & Doodle Engine Update

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
### 🚀 v3.5.0 - The Canvas & Outliner Update

This major update transforms the Pinboard from a simple note compiler into a full-fledged spatial thinking and outliner tool. We've introduced native Obsidian Canvas generation and quick Mindmap exports!

**🎨 Native Canvas Generation (The Evidence Board):**
* **1-Click Mindmaps:** Hit the new `🎨 Canvas` button in your Pinboard to instantly generate a native Obsidian `.canvas` file.
* **Smart Spatial Layout:** The plugin automatically calculates the geometry to lay out your notes perfectly. Titles become root nodes, marginalias become child nodes (with clickable links to their source), and the engine extracts the **exact raw paragraph context** as sub-branches.

**🌳 Mindmap Quick-Export (Optimized for Excalidraw):**
* **Visual Hierarchy:** You can now indent and outdent your pinned notes using the new `←` and `→` arrows, creating a parent-child structure right inside the sidebar.
* **Clipboard Integration:** Clicking the `📋 Clip` button instantly compiles your structured board into a clean Markdown outline and copies it directly to your system clipboard—perfect for pasting straight into Excalidraw's Mindmap Builder or any other outliner.

**🛡️ Pinboard Refinements & Bug Fixes:**
* **Empty Board Support:** Fixed an issue where the title creation input wouldn't appear if the Pinboard was completely empty.
* **Seamless Reordering:** Disabled the stitching Drag & Drop engine while inside the Pinboard tab, finally allowing you to freely reorder and reorganize your pinned marginalias without accidental merging.
releases.name: v4.2.4 -  The Canvas & Outliner Update

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
### 🚀 v4.2.3 - The Pinboard 

This major update introduces the **Pinboard**, a powerful new way to structure and compile your study notes before exporting them, alongside crucial stability fixes and a sleek, minimalist UI overhaul.

**📌 The Pinboard (Your Compilation Canvas):**
* **Collect on the fly:** Hover over any note or group in the Explorer and click the new `○` icon to pin it to your Board.
* **Custom Titles & Structure:** Add Markdown headers (e.g., `## My amazing title`) directly inside the Board tab.
* **Drag & Drop Reordering:** Freely drag and drop your pinned notes and titles within the Board to build the perfect structure for your summary.
* **Advanced Context Export:** Clicking "Export to Note" no longer just copies your marginalias. The engine now travels to the original files, extracts the **exact raw paragraph** associated with your note, and compiles a beautifully formatted Markdown study guide.

**✨ Minimalist UI Overhaul:**
* Replaced colorful emojis with clean and typographic symbols (`⟳`, `⌇`, `⛓︎`, `🗁`) that blend perfectly with Obsidian's dark and light themes.


**🛡️ Critical Stability & Bug Fixes:**
* **Image Regex Armor:** Fixed a critical bug where adding spaces in image tags (e.g., `img: [[file.png]]`) would break the stitching engine and corrupt the Markdown file. Images now render and stitch flawlessly.
releases.name: v4.2.3 - - The Pinboard

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
### 🚀 v4.2.2 - The Explorer  & Layout Fix Update

This update turns the Marginalia Explorer into a true command center for your Zettelkasten and introduces a brand-new official tool to fix Obsidian's block-breaking layout limitations!

**✨ New Features:**
* **👀 X-Ray Vision (Context Hover):** You no longer need to open a file to remember what a marginalia was about. Simply hover your mouse over any note in the Explorer for half a second. A non-intrusive floating tooltip will appear, showing you the exact source paragraph from your markdown file with the relevant line highlighted.
* **🗂️ Group by Content (Deduplication):** Is your sidebar cluttered with 15 instances of the exact same marginalia? Click the new **Group** button. The plugin will instantly group all identical marginalias into a single, clean, collapsible folder showing the number of occurrences.
* **🔗 Mass Stitching (Multi-Stitch):** Drag & Drop just got leveled up. You can now drag a grouped folder of notes and drop it onto a target note (or vice versa). The plugin will automatically stitch *all* of them together in one go! A native safety confirmation prompt has been added to prevent accidental mass-linking.

**🛠️ The `[!cornell]` Invisible Block:**
* **The Problem:** Obsidian treats paragraphs and lists as completely separate blocks, which causes Reading View to push lists out of alignment when placing a margin note next to an introductory paragraph.
* **The Solution:** We've introduced the `[!cornell]` callout! Wrap your paragraph and list together using this blockquote syntax, **and place your marginalia tag just above it**. The plugin will make the callout box completely invisible, allowing your text to flow naturally while forcing Obsidian to treat it as a single block. Your marginalia will now perfectly embrace the entire group!
releases.name: v4.2.2 - The Explorer & Layout Fix Update

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
his is a massive structural update that brings images to your margins, completely overhauls how Reading View handles stacking, and introduces an incredibly fluid way to build Zettelkasten threads.

**✨ New Features:**
* **🖼️ Multimedia Marginalia:** You can now embed images directly in your margins! Use the new `img:[[filename.ext]]` syntax inside your `%%> %%` tags. 

  * **Hover Zoom:** Embedded images perfectly fit the margin width and feature a beautiful magnifying zoom effect when you hover over them.
* **🖱️ Drag & Drop Stitching:** Building knowledge threads is now as easy as a flick of the wrist. In the Marginalia Explorer sidebar, simply click and drag one note over another to instantly link them together. The target note becomes the "parent," and the dragged note becomes the "child."
* **👀 Hover Peeks:** Hovering over the `🔗` link icon in your marginalia will now trigger Obsidian's native page preview popup, letting you read connected thoughts without leaving your current note.

**🛠️ Architectural Overhaul (Reading View):**
* **Independent Containers:** The Reading View engine has been rebuilt from scratch. Paragraphs and their marginalia now live in absolute structural containers.
* **Flawless Vertical Stacking:** Multiple notes on the exact same paragraph now stack perfectly downwards *without* pushing the next paragraph out of alignment. Empty lines are now fully respected.
releases.name: v4.2.1 - The Multimedia, Stacking & Drag-and-Drop Update

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
🚀 - The Stacking & Bidirectional Update

✨ New Features:

    Bidirectional Margins: You can now use the %%< syntax to force a specific note to render on the opposite margin. Perfect for dual-margin workflows! (Huge thanks to @Sirnii for the feature request).

🐛 Bug Fixes & Improvements:

    Smart Stacking: Multiple marginalia within the same paragraph no longer overlap. They now stack vertically in a clean column.

    Gravity & Alignment Fix: Fixed an issue where notes written at the end of a paragraph would align too low. Notes are now always injected at the top-level line of the text block, maintaining a perfect layout regardless of tag placement.
releases.name: v4.1.1 - The Stacking & Bidirectional Update

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
### 🧵 Margin Threads (Zettelkasten in the Margins) 

Why limit your connections to main text? You can now stitch your marginalia notes together across your entire vault to create independent, multi-level thought threads.

* **The Stitch Button:** Easily connect a note from one file to a note in another using the Sidebar. 
* **Recursive Tree View:** The "Threads" tab automatically renders infinite-level hierarchical trees of your connected notes. Follow a concept down the rabbit hole!
* **Smart Focus & Filters:** Click on a color pill (e.g., Yellow for 'Questions') to instantly filter your threads. The plugin is smart enough to show you the exact parent note your filtered concept came from, preserving your context.
* **Native & Future-Proof:** Threads use hidden, native Obsidian Block IDs (`[[Note#^id]]`). Your connections will survive file renames and will even show up in your native Obsidian Graph View!
releases.name: v4.1.0  -Margin Threads (Zettelkasten in the Margins)

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
### 🚀 Drag & Drop Integration (Excalidraw & Canvas) 

Marginalia is no longer just for reading; it's a tool for connecting ideas! 

You can now click and drag any note directly from the **Marginalia Explorer** sidebar and drop it into an Obsidian Canvas or an Excalidraw drawing. 
* The plugin instantly creates a native, indestructible Block ID link behind the scenes.
* Clicking the dropped node in your canvas will open the source file and scroll you down to the exact paragraph where the note belongs. Perfect for building mind maps from your summaries!
releases.name: v4.0.2 -  Drag & Drop Integration (Excalidraw & Canvas)

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
. 🖨️ PDF Export & Printing Support 

Obsidian's native PDF export engine notoriously struggles with floating margin notes. To solve this, Cornell Marginalia includes a dedicated "Print Engine" to guarantee your summaries look perfect and professional on paper or tablets.

**How to export your notes to PDF:**
1. **Prepare:** Open the Command Palette (`Ctrl/Cmd + P`) and run **"Prepare Marginalia for PDF Print"**. Your `%%>` notes will temporarily transform into safe HTML tags. 
2. **Export:** Use Obsidian's native "Export to PDF" feature. Your marginalia will automatically align in a clean, classic left-column Cornell layout, and the main text will wrap perfectly around them.
3. **Restore:** Once your PDF is saved, run **"Restore Marginalia after PDF Print"** to instantly return your text to its original, clean Markdown state.

> ⚠️ **Important:** Always remember to run the *Restore* command after exporting to keep your Markdown files clean and future-proof!
releases.name: v4.0.1 -  PDF Export & Printing Support

releases.description:
## 🧠 The "Active Study" Update is Here!

This release transforms Cornell Marginalia from a note-taking tool into a **full-fledged study system** for Obsidian.

### ✨ New Feature: Active Recall Mode (Blur) 🙈
We added a new **"Eye" icon** 👁️ to the left ribbon.
* **Click it** to toggle Active Recall Mode.
* Any margin note ending in `;;` (e.g., `%%> What is the powerhouse? ;; %%`) will automatically **blur** the associated paragraph.
* **Hover** over the blurred text to reveal the answer. Perfect for self-testing!

### 🃏 New Feature: Flashcard Generator
Turn your notes into Anki/Spaced Repetition cards instantly!
1. Write your notes using the format: `Answer %%> Question ;; %%`.
2. Run the command **"Flashcards Generation"**.
3. The plugin will automatically append a `### Flashcards` section at the bottom of your note with the format `Question :: Answer`.
* *Smart Update:* It detects existing flashcards to avoid duplicates!

---
*Happy Studying! 🩺*
releases.name: v3.1.1 - The "Active Study" Update 🧠 (Blur and Flashcards)

releases.author: latazadehomero
releases.description:
## 🎨 The "Semantic Color" Update is here!

This update transforms Cornell Marginalia from a layout tool into a **powerful study system**. You can now color-code your marginal notes based on their meaning!

### ✨ New Features: Semantic Highlighting
Now, if you start your note with a specific symbol (prefix), the margin note will change color automatically.

**Default Prefixes included:**
* `?` → **Orange** (Perfect for Questions/Doubts)
* `!` → **Yellow** (Important Concepts)
* `X-` → **Red** (Corrections/Mistakes)
* `V-` → **Green** (Reviewed/Verified)

**Example:**
* Write `%%> ? What is the mitochondria? %%` to get an **Orange** note.
* Write `%%> ! Powerhouse of the cell %%` to get a **Yellow** note.
* Write `%%> Normal note %%` to keep using your theme's default accent color.

### ⚙️ Full Customization
Don't like the defaults? No problem!
* Go to **Settings > Cornell Marginalia** to create your own system.
* **Add new tags:** Create a "#" tag for definitions in Blue, or a "@" tag for references in Purple.
* **Change colors:** Pick any color from the color picker.
* **Remove tags:** Clean up what you don't use.

### 🔧 Improvements
* Optimized rendering engine for color injection.
* Fully compatible with the alignment (Left/Right) and font settings introduced in v1.4.0.

---
*Happy Studying! 🩺🐧*
releases.name: v2.0.0 - Semantic Colors & Custom Tags 🎨
